Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to generate a list of targeted open ended questions based on the provided long process description about property onboarding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The goal is to clarify and improve understanding of the process by uncovering missing details understanding decision criteria clarifying roles responsibilities verifying timing sequencing and learning about exception handling

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key aspects of the process described 1 Initial contact and documentation review property coordinator 2 Database entry of property details 3 Property manager assignment based on workload geography experience track record 4 Introductory meeting between manager and landlord 5 Photography and inspection scheduling 6 Addressing inspection issues 7 Marketing creation and pricing updates 8 Property activation in systems 9 Tenant screening and selection including competitive scenarios 10 Lease finalization and move in scheduling 11 Quality checks and audits compliance financial IT 12 Ongoing communication tracking across stakeholders

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions should Be open ended Not request SQL or implementation details Focus on conceptual understanding Cover missing details decision criteria roles responsibilities timing sequencing exceptions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should organize these questions logically perhaps following the flow of the process or by category Categorizing by theme might be more effective for the interviewer to ensure comprehensive coverage

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Categories to cover 1 Documentation and Initial Screening Step 1 2 2 Resource Allocation and Assignment Step 3 3 Inspection and Preparation Step 5 6 4 Marketing and Activation Step 7 8 5 Tenant Selection and Lease Execution Step 9 10 6 Governance and Quality Assurance Step 11 7 Cross Cutting Concerns Communication exceptions timing metrics

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Let me draft specific questions for each

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Documentation and Initial What happens if a landlord cannot provide specific documents for example lost deed Is there an escalation path How does the coordinator verify authenticity of documents What constitutes outdated for inspection reports

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Assignment How is workload measured Number of properties units complexity score What happens if no manager has the right geographic specialization How are unique requirements categorized and matched Who has final say in the assignment decision

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Inspection and Prep What is the typical turnaround time between scheduling and actual inspection What severity levels exist for inspection issues Which ones block progress versus can be fixed later Who bears the cost of repairs landlord or company What happens if the landlord refuses to make required repairs

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Marketing and Activation How is pricing determined Algorithm committee individual judgment What happens if market trends shift during the onboarding process How long does the marketing preparation typically take What determines which platforms properties get brochures versus just online listings

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Tenant Selection What criteria differentiate suitable from unsuitable tenants beyond standard credit background checks How are conflicts resolved when multiple tenants are interested What is the negotiation authority of the property manager for discounts What happens if no suitable tenants apply within a certain timeframe

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Governance At what point do compliance and financial reviews occur Before or after lease signing What happens if compliance or finance flags issues post lease signing How are audit findings tracked and resolved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cross cutting What is the typical total duration from initial contact to tenant move in What communication protocols exist when the process stalls How are custom lease clauses vetted What special insurance scenarios exist and how are they handled How is knowledge transferred if a property manager leaves mid process What metrics measure success of this onboarding process What is the most common bottleneck or failure point

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I should refine these to be truly open ended not leading and to encourage storytelling examples I should also ensure they do not sound like they are asking for technical implementation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Good open ended format Walk me through how Can you describe What factors influence How do you handle What happens when

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Avoid Do you use a database to Is the process automated How many days does too specific on metrics unless asking about variability

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually asking about typical duration is fine but better to ask about variability How much does the timeline vary between different property types

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me organize the final list into logical sections for the interviewer
